properties.remark: AROUND 1115 AM, PUBLIC POSTED A VIDEO ON TWITTER SHOWING BRIEF PERIOD OF ZERO VISIBILITY ALONG HIGHWAY 160 NEAR BASIN AVE IN PAHRUMP.
CHP REPORTED ZERO VISIBILITY WITH CARS PULLED OVER ON SHOULDER IN MULTIPLE LOCATIONS. OCCURRED BETWEEN 0845-0945.
VISIBILITY DOWN TO ESTIMATED 50 FEET ON ROUTE 66 BETWEEN KINGMAN AIRPORT AND VALLEY VISTA.
FLASH FLOODING RESULTING IN ROAD DAMAGE ALONG HIGHWAY 168 BETWEEN ASPENDELL AND BISHOP. TIME OF REPORT 1600. TIME OF FLOODING ESTIMATED TO BE 1100.
HACKBERRY ROAD IS CLOSED FROM AUSTIN PEAK TO US 66 DUE TO FLOODING.
WATER FLOWING OVER SPRING MOUNTAIN RANCH ROAD AT SPRING MOUNTAIN RANCH PARK.
SOUTH BLAKE RANCH ROAD IS CLOSED FROM MILE POST 15 TO US 93 DUE TO FLOODING.
FLOODING CLOSED BOTH DIRECTIONS OF HIGHWAY 159.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE TO CARPORTS, TREES, AND ROOFS.
J STREET OFFRAMP FLOODED AT INTERSTATE 40 EASTBOUND. CALTRANS PLACED FLOODED SIGNS ON BOTH SIDES OF THE ROAD.
FLOODING ON THE CASINO FLOOR OF TROPICANA CASINO IN LAUGHLIN.
BULLHEAD CITY GOVERNMENT REPORTED SIGNIFICANT FLOODING ON HIGHWAY 95 AT 7TH STREET. AS OF 1751 ALL ROADS ARE OPEN.
ROADWAY FLOODING AT NEEDLES HIGHWAY AND RIVER ROAD.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS MULTIPLE SWIFT WATER RESCUES.
ROADWAY FLOODING ALONG NATIONAL TRAILS HIGHWAY IN AMBOY. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
FLOODING IN LAUGHLIN ALONG BRUCE WOODBURY DRIVE. AS OF 1820 WATER HAS RECEDED BUT A LOT OF DEBRIS IS LEFT ON THE ROADWAY.
FLOODING ALONG NEEDLES HIGHWAY AND BRUCE WOODBURY DRIVE IN LAUGHLIN. AS OF 1800 WATER HAD RECEDED AND LEFT A LOT OF DEBRIS ON THE ROADWAY. TIME OF FLOODING ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
HARD CLOSURE OF HIGHWAY 95 AT AIRPORT ROAD DUE TO ROADWAY FLOODING.
WATER FLOWING OVER BRUCE WOODBURY HEADING TOWARD CASINO DRIVE IN LAUGHLIN.
FLOODING FROM THE MEADOW VALLEY WASH OVER WARM SPRINGS ROAD IN MOAPA. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
HIGHWAY 95 AT MILE MARKER 50. ROADWAY IS FLOODED. SEMI TRUCKS CAN GET THROUGH BUT SEVERAL VEHICLES ARE STOPPED.
ROADWAY FLOODING AT INTERSTATE 15 SOUTHBOUND AT LAKE DOLORES.
LOTS OF DEBRIS ON THE ROAD AT STATE ROUTE 127 AT SHOSHONE. 6 INCHES BY 100 FEET OF SMALL ROCKS ON THE ROAD.
ROADWAY FLOODING AT FIVE MILE ROAD ONRAMP AT INTERSTATE 40 EASTBOUND.
HIGHWAY 160 IS FLOODED IN PAHRUMP.
FLOODED ROADWAYS IN LAUGHLIN NEAR CASINO DRIVE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS THAT WEST BOUND MAIN STREET IS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE TO CARPORTS, TREES, AND ROOFS.
COUNTY ROUTE 1 CLOSED FROM MILE MARKER 1 TO MILE MARKER 5 DUE TO FLOODING. ROAD WAS REOPENED AT 0225.
PUMP STATION ROAD IS CLOSED FROM US 93 TO BACK ROAD DUE TO FLOODING.
FLOODED PARKING LOT AND PARKING GARAGE OF EDGEWATER CASINO IN LAUGHLIN.
HIGHWAY 95 IS COMPLETELY WASHED OUT. HARD CLOSURE STARTING AT INTERSTATE 40.
PHOTOS SHOWED FLOOD DEBRIS, STUCK VEHICLE, AND ERODED ROADWAY ON OATMAN HIGHWAY AT WARM SPRINGS WASH.
INDIAN HEAD DRIVE CLOSED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF MCCORMICK BOULEVARD DUE TO FLOOD DAMAGE. TIME OF FLOODING ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
BENEGAS RANCH ROAD IS CLOSED FROM US 93 TO BENEGAS RANCH DUE TO FLOODING.
ANTARES ROAD IS CLOSED FROM CALIFORNIA DRIVE TO PIERCE FERRY ROAD DUE TO FLOODING.
OATMAN HIGHWAY IS CLOSED FROM TULE DRIVE TO INTERSTATE 40 DUE TO FLOODING.

THE AREA 27 ARL SORD SITE BEGAN MEASURING THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS GREATER THAN 58 MPH AT 1715 PDT. THESE GUSTS CONTINUED THROUGH 1730 PDT. THE PEAK GUST MEASURED DURING THIS TIME WAS 97 MPH, WHICH OCCURRED AT 1715 PDT.
AT 1315 PDT, THE AREA 5 NORTH ARL SORD SENSOR RECORDED A GUST OF 60 MPH.
VIDEO SHOWING BLOWING DUST WITH VISIBILITY LESS THAN A QUARTER OF A MILE SOUTHWEST OF BAKER. TIME ESTIMATED BY SATELLITE.

AT 1432 PDT, CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED HEAVY RAINFALL WITH EROSION OCCURRING ON BOTH SIDES OF THE FREEWAY. CIMA ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. AT LEAST ONE VEHICLE WAS STUCK IN A WASH WITH WATER UP TO THE BOTTOM OF THE DOOR.
AT 1545, ARL SORD STATION 38 RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 76 MPH.
AT 1615 PDT, THE ARL SORD SENSOR LOCATED AT AREA 5 NORTH RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 64 MPH FROM THE NNW.
AT 1615 PDT, THE ARL SORD SENSOR LOCATED AT AREA 5 NORTH RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 64 MPH FROM THE NNW.
AT 1700 PDT, THE ARL SORD SENSOR LOCATED AT AREA 27 RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 93 MPH FROM THE ESE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS MULTIPLE VEHICLES STUCK IN OR NEAR ARTISTS PALETTE IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK.
CALTRANS DISTRICT 9 REPORTED HIGHWAY 178 CLOSED DUE TO ROADWAY FLOODING EAST OF JUNCTION WITH HIGHWAY 127. TIME ESTIMATED OFF SATELLITE.
RED MOUNTAIN RAWS RECORDED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 58 MPH. ELEVATION 3661 FEET.
AT 1951 PDT, THE RED MOUNTAIN RAWS RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 58 MPH FROM THE SOUTHWEST.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS THAT A VEHICLE IS STUCK IN A FLOODED ROADWAY. ROAD IS UNACCUSABLE DUE TO FLOODING.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS FLOODING RESULTING IN VEHICLES BEING WASHED AWAY.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A VEHICLE STUCK IN WATER ON SR 190 AND THE ROAD POSSIBLY WASHED OUT. CHP UNIT UNABLE TO CROSS A NEARBY SECTION DUE TO WATER AND DEBRIS ON ROADWAY.
AT 11:15 PDT, THE ARL SORD SENSOR LOCATED AT REBOUND MEDA RECORDED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 69 MPH FROM THE SOUTH.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROADWAY FLOODING AND DEBRIS OVER THE ROAD, RESULTING IN A VEHICLE SPINOUT.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS A MUDSLIDE AND FLOODING ON THE I-15 SOUTHBOUND AND EAST MAIN STREET OFF RAMP.
At 1655 and 1657 PDT, the BYS ASOS recorded thunderstorm wind gusts of 61 mph.
A mesonet site at I-15 and Moapa Valley Road measured a thunderstorm wind gust to 60 mph.
The Area 27 mesonet station located within the Nevada National Security Site measured a thunderstorm wind gust to 69 mph.
NV DOT sensor APXNV at Interstate 15 Southbound at Highway 93 measured a wind gust of 58 mph from a nearby thunderstorm.
Several sites in Henderson by Water Street have observed between 58 and 64 mph wind gusts from a nearby thunderstorm.

Severe flash flood impacts reported by emergency management in Fish Lake Valley in Esmeralda County. Flood impacts to Dyer Elementary School and the Community Center. Severe agricultural impacts noted. Portions of Highway 264 and 266 through Dyer and Oasis have limited access due to water over the road. Time of flooding estimated by radar.
At approximately 6:15 pm PDT, the Nevershine RAWS recorded a thunderstorm wind gust of 61 mph. Time estimated using radar.
At 8:11 pm PDT, the sensor at West Career and Tech Academy recorded a thunderstorm wind gust of 58 mph. Data courtesy of Earth Networks.
Water running over paved road in Red Rock Canyon with considerable rock debris over roadway. Exact location unknown. Time estimated from radar.
Large tree uprooted in the vicinity of US Highway 95 and Craig Road. Time estimated from radar.
Water over roadway at Lake Meade Boulevard and Anasazi Drive. Landscaping rocks and debris also in roadway.
Rapid-onset flooding of Short Creek closed Hildale Street for one hour.

NWS Employee reports significant runoff and flash flooding in the Lanfair Valley area of the Mojave National Preserve. Flash flooding and runoff resulted in debris over area roads, including Ivanpah Road and New York Mountain Road.
California Highway Patrol reports mud, rock, and debris over the road.
Corrects previous rain report from 6 NNE Valentine. Heavy rain carried some rocks onto Route 66 near mile marker 91. Began 135 pm and estimated end time at 210 pm.
Needles Airport ASOS reported a peak wind gust of 59 mph.

A landspout tornado was reported along the Mojave river near Barstow. Several spotter photos confirmed the landspout but no damage or injuries were reported from local officials. Timing estimated by radar and spotter reports.
Several reports of roadway flooding on WAZE. Reports were on State Route 62, between Sunfair Rd. and Rotary Way. Began 521 pm with estimated end time 605 pm.

California Highway Patrol and gas station attendant at Fort Cady Rd and I-40 both confirmed the visibility dropped to near zero in blowing dust. Reduction in visibility caused a multi-vehicle accident with 10 to 20 vehicles involved which closed Interstate 40. Near zero visibility estimated start at 540 pm with estimated ending at 6 pm PDT.

Pierce Ferry Road web camera operated by Mohave County Flood Control District captured blowing dust and reduced visibility. Estimated near zero visibility from the images. Estimated start time was 537 pm MST and ending at 605 pm MST.
Spotter in east Bullhead City reported a peak wind gust to 59 with a passing thunderstorm.
Flash flooding observed at Canyon Diablo Road and Hunting Hawk in Las Vegas.
South Oatman Highway is closed from East Tule Drive to Interstate 40 due to flooding. Time of flooding estimated by radar.

Trained spotter reports thunderstorm wind damage. A 16 foot by 8 foot aluminum canopy was flipped up and on top of the building, completely destroying the canopy. Spotter also reported multiple power lines down in northern Kingman. Wind gust magnitude estimated to be around 55 mph.
National Parks Service reports docks separating at Lake Mead due to strong winds and waves. Wind gust magnitude estimated to be around 50-55 mph.
West Agua Fria Drive is closed from North Milky Way Road to North Estrella Road due to flooding.
West 7th Street is closed from North Sandra Drive to North Mineral Road due to flooding. Time estimated from radar.
West 1st Street is closed from North Agua Mawsa Drive to North Sandra Drive due to flooding. Time estimated by radar.
North Estrella Road closed from West Chino Drive to West Estrella Circle due to flooding.
Goffs Road is closed from Interstate 40 to Highway 95 due to flooding.
Roadway flooding reported on Highway 95 at Paiute Wash.
Arizona Department of Transportation reports flooding on Route 66.
Road closure at River Road Cutoff / Park Road off of Interstate 40 north of Needles closed due to flooding.
Tweeds Point RAWS site in northern Arizona observed a sustained wind speed of 73 mph and a gust of 61 mph, coupled with a 10 degree temperature drop and a 44% relative humidity rise in an hour.
West Cottonwood Road is closed from mile marker 2 to mile marker 16 due to flooding.
Local Emergency Manager reports that Highway 62 is closed due to flooding from the State Route 177 junction to 2.9 miles east of Twentynine Palms.
West 7th Street is closed from North Sandra Drive to North Mineral Road due to flooding.
West 1st Street is closed from North Agua Mawsa Drive to North Sandra Drive due to flooding.
California Highway Patrol reports roadway flooding and debris across Highway 190 between mile marker 45-46.
California Highway Patrol reports a road washout at the junction of State Route 190 and State Route 136.
Department of Highways reports roadway flooding at US-395 and Boulder Creek.
California Highway Patrol reports flooding in the second south bound lane.
California Highway Patrol reports rocks and debris blocking the roadway.
Eastbound Ute Road is closed at northbound Interstate 15 due to flooding.
California Highway Patrol reports that State Route 190 is flooded from Pearls Dip to Stovepipe Wells.
Heavy flooding pushing debris across the roadway at State Route 62 between Cactus Jack to Godwin. From CHP.
State Route 62 eastbound just east of Olympic has mud and debris on the roadway.
Yucca Mesa Road at Barron Drive closed due to flooding. Report via Facebook.
Weather-related closure. National Trails Highway (66) west is closed due to heavy rains and flooding on 66 between Essex Road / Sunflower Springs Road and Kelbaker Road. Report via Twitter.
Joe Davis between Utah and Sherman Hoyt is closed due to flooding.
Baghdad between Amboy and Valle Vista is closed due to flooding.
Ocotillo between Gorgonio and Veterans Way is closed due to flooding.
Split Rock between Buena Vista and Bagely is closed due to flooding.
Tamarisk between Buena Vista and Bagley is closed due to flooding.
Rock slid off mountain and into roadway at SR62 and Hess Rd.
State Route 190 remains closed due to flooding. There is water flowing over the roadway.
Water is reaching vehicle hoods.
Roadway is flooding.
Roadway flooded. Multiple vehicles stuck.
Mudflow covering La Contenta Road and Alta Loma Road.
Roadway possibly washed out.
Multiple vehicles getting stuck.
Mud and debris on roadway.
Mud, dirt, and rock slide.
Roadway flooding.
Fast moving water across roadway at Fort Irwin Road and Interstate 15.
All of Highway 395 from Bishop south to Pearsonville has been closed due to flooding impacts. Report from CHP.
State Road 247 at Pipes Canyon Road was reported flooded and not passable. The road was closed due to the flooding.
Mount Charleston Fire Chief reported several washed out roads, heavy flows of water, and damage to the Mount Charleston Fire Station due to flooding.

Two feet of rock and debris was reported across all lanes of SR 62 near Valley View Road. Small boulders were in the roadway from Senelis Ave to east of Valley View Road. Law enforcement was working to close the eastbound lanes of SR 62.
Inyo National Forecast observed rock and debris deposited across Whitney Portal Parking lots by fast flowing water overnight. Estimated start time midnight with estimated end time noon.

Inyo National Forecast observed the road to Lone Pine Campground off Whiney Portal Road damaged by fast flowing water. Rock and debris also on the road. Estimated start time midnight with estimated end time at 900 am.
Video from the public shows multiple feet of water flowing down the mountain.
Emergency manager reports flooding on Hafen Ranch Road.
Nevada Department of Transportation closed Nevada State Route 157 in both directions at mile marker 12 due to flash flooding.
Local media report of a water rescue near Koval Lane and East Flamingo Road. Fire department was responding to calls of a man and woman washed away in the flood canal near there.
Clark County Fire Department reports a water rescue at the intersection of Maryland Parkway and Desert Inn Road.
Local newspaper reported that police attempted to rescue a person from flood waters in the wash near Nellis and Sahara, but were unsuccessful. Said person was last reported as missing.

*** 1 FATAL *** 1 body recovered following flooding in Las Vegas on Wednesday evening. The person was swept into the wash during the storm and was found in the Flamingo Wash near Nellis Blvd two days later. One additional person is still missing.

Overturned big rig resulting in the closure of 3 lanes. Heavy rain and wind were reported with this report. Dispatch was unclear if it was a blow over or hydroplane situation. Later social media post showed powerlines blown down as well near the interstate, thus it seems reasonable to assume the big rig was blown over.
Sensor at Allegiant Stadium gusted to 65 mph at 640pm due to thunderstorm winds.
Snapped tree at the Neon Museum near North Las Vegas Boulevard and McWilliams Avenue.
Mesquite police reported a powerline down on Hafen Lane at the intersection of Arrowhead.
Tree snapped in half.
Winds of 58 mph or more lasted from 0109-0122 MST.
Time of occurrence estimated from radar and lightning data.
Legend Ranch Road was closed from Highway 93 to Burro Drive.
Chino Dr was closed from Milky Way Rd to Estrella Rd, Egar Rd was closed from Agua Fria Dr to Chinle Dr, and Shinarump Dr was closed from Garnet Rd to Aztec Rd. Reports came in between 0400 and 0500.
High winds lasted from 0406 to 0506.
Antares Road was closed from Pierce Ferry Road to California Drive.
Hackberry Road closed from US93 to Austin Peak due to flooding.
About a foot of water across Highway 95 at mile marker 60.
A sinkhole was opened on northbound Jones Blvd south of Flamingo Road which exposed a gas pipeline.
Emergency manager reports mud and debris across Dawson Ave.

Various reports of flash flood impacts across Las Vegas. Reports included road closures at Alexander Road and Puebla Street, Charleston Blvd and Commerce Street, Las Vegas Blvd at Cheyenne. Highway 95 northbound was down to one lane due to debris. Multiple cars were stalled in flood waters in these locations.
Report of Sahara and Decatur intersection flooded resulting in cars submerged in flood waters. Report from KSNV.
Flooding on ramp from Flamingo to I-515 northbound. All lanes closed.
Pump Station Road closed from US93 to Back Road due to flooding.
Huffritter Road closed from US93 to Back Road due to flooding.
Bogels Ranch Road closed from Back Road to Upper Trout Creek due to flooding.
Banagas Road is closed from Highway 93 to Back Road due to weather.
Road closed due to flooding in all directions at E. Cheyenne Ave. LV Blvd N.
Valley of Fire State Park experienced flooding on the west side of the park via Valley of Fire Highway. Road is unpassable.
Nevada Department of Highways reported a road closure on South Moapa Valley Boulevard in both directions near Lewis Avenue due to flooding.
The KBYS AWOS reported a peak wind gust of 83 mph due to a nearby thunderstorm.
Social media report showing video of street flooding in east Las Vegas. Location estimated by radar.
Large tree down in Mountains Edge near Gomer and El Capitan.
CHP reported a flash flood along State Route 62. Vehicle will not accelerate due to water in the undercarriage.
Measured wind gust of 62 mph. Data courtesy of EarthNetworks.
CHP reports roadway flooding. An update at 1622 included vehicles stuck on the roadway between mile marker 10 and Vidal Junction.
CHP reports roadway flooding on Highway 95 near Vidal Junction at Mile Marker 20.
CHP reported heavy rain and flooding in the roadway at State Route 62 and Ironage Road.
FOX5 television broadcast reported Boulder Highway and Nellis Blvd flooded with multiple cars stranded at the intersection in flood waters.
Flooding at Eastern and Bonanza.
Webcam showed flooding over the roadway at Blue Diamond and Rainbow.
Flooded roadway at Tropicana Avenue east of Rainbow Boulevard.
CHP reports 5 vehicles stuck between 2 wash out spots on State Route 127. Whole road is washing out. Roadway is caving in.
CHP reports flooding over Dumont Dunes Road.
CHP reports vehicles stuck between closure and Vidal Road. Roadway wash out in the area.
CHP reports flooding and debris in the roadway at State Route 62 at mile marker 43.
Emergency manager reports flooding at Bell Vista Road and Ash Meadows Road.
Emergency manager reports highway 160 south of Tecopa Road is flooded impacting traffic.
Signal Road is closed from Highway 93 to Alamo Road due to flooding.
Flooding along the roadway at Fort Apache and Ford.
CHP reports roadway flooding at State Route 62 and Midland.
CHP reports standing water flooding on Mountain View Street. Mud is across the roadway from previous flowing water. Roadway needs to be plowed.
CHP reported State Route 127 is closed at Silver Lane due to flooding.
Union Pacific sensor in Moore measured a thunderstorm related wind gust of 59 mph.
CHP reported a vehicle stuck in flood waters on Kelbaker Road.
CHP reported roadway flooding along Highway 95 at mile marker 66.
Significant water flowing through the Linq Parking Garage.
Signal Road is closed from Highway 93 to Alamo Road due to flooding.
CHP reported rain and flooding at Tecopa at Old Spanish Trail Highway and Carpenter Road.
CHP reported a vehicle stuck in flood waters on Highway 95 at mile marker 59.
6 inches to a foot of flowing water over the roadway at Fort Apache and Sahara.
Highway 160 between Las Vegas and Pahrump is flooded with mud over the roadway.
Webcam shows water and mud over Oatman Highway in Topock where Sacramento Wash intersects the road.

Emergency manager reported 6 swift water rescues needed at Highway 160 at Mile Marker 34. These rescues included two first responders. A sudden rush of flood water overcame recovery efforts that were ongoing at the time.
CHP reported major debris in the roadway. State Route 62 is closed due to flooding near Valle View.
Nevada Highway 159 is closed from Sky Vista Drive to Highway 160 due to flooding.
Ranch Road in Moapa closed due to flooding.
Water and mud over the roadway on Calico Basin Road at Red Rock Canyon.
County Highway 91 is closed from mile marker 7 to mile marker 3 due to flooding.
NDOT reports Highway 95 closed due to flooding from Paiute Way to Lee Canyon Road.
Hidden Valley Road closed due to flooding from Interstate 15 to Highway 168.
Alamo Road closed from Signal Road to Milepost 56 due to flooding.
CHP reports a rockslide just north of the Windmill Resort. Roads also reported washed out in the area.
Water and debris flowing over Warm Springs at Tenaya.
Water flowing over Warm Springs at Buffalo.
Intersection of Sahara and Arville flooded with water half way up car tires.
Intersection of Sunset at Sandhill flooded and impassable.
Flooding on Hacienda between Coyote Lake and Harvard. Road is impassable.
Intersection of Sahara and McLeod flooded with the water up to car windows.
Intersection of Flamingo and Boulder Highway flooded with high water rescues ongoing.
Intersection of McLeod and Desert Inn flooded with high water rescues ongoing.
Intersection of Sahara and Walnut flooded with high water rescues ongoing.
Intersection of Flamingo and Pecos flooded with vechile stuck.
Intersection of Harmon and Lamb flooded with high water rescues ongoing.
Intersection of Lake Mead and Mt Hood flooded with several vehicles disabled by flood waters.
Flooding at the intersection of SR62 and US95. Over two dozen vehicles reported stuck.
Flash Flooding occuring along Short Creek in Colorado City.
Water and debris flowing across Lake Mead Blvd at Pabco Rd.
SR 169 at Lewis St in Overton closed due to flooding.
Water flowing across US 95 at several locations.
Water flowing across the road at several locations on Goffs Rd near I-40.
Car disabled in flood waters at Lake Mead Blvd and Sloan Ln.
Flooding with water across all lanes of I-15 near the California Nevada State line.

Hildale Fire Dispatch reported flooding across Hildale and Colorado City. All creek crossing roads were closed for 2-3 hours during the storm yesterday with the creek out of its banks and flash flooding. The Hildale Street creek crossing was heavily damaged and will likely be closed for some time. Time estimated via streamflow observations.
Swift water rescue reported at Stinnet St and Pyle in Henderson.
Swift Water Rescue reported at Eastern and Serene Ave in Henderson.
Swift Water Rescue reported near Lone Pine and Laurel Ave in Henderson.

National Parks Service employees reported a dust storm moving south through Death Valley National Park. At 1745 PDT, the dust storm was approaching Cow Creek. Visibility dropped to 250 yards as the dust rolled through.
Social media video showed blowing dust reducing visibility to less than 100 feet at times along Interstate 15.
CHP reports flooding at SR-62 and Yucca Park Road with water flowing down the Morongo Grade.
